correctionsMade.issue: Tone was not one of the seven reading-skills topics named in the current ETS outline, and the stimulus did not assess fact versus opinion.
correctionsMade.change: Replaced the tone task with a four-sentence fact-versus-opinion item while retaining answer position 3 and the school-transportation context.

correctionsMade.issue: The keyed main-idea choice inferred sensitivity to broadly “intense” environments beyond what the passage directly established.
correctionsMade.change: Narrowed the keyed choice and its rationale to the reported quiet hour, families who avoided crowded exhibits, and reduced access barriers.

correctionsMade.issue: The source-type comparison was an extension beyond the explicit current ETS reading-skills list, while this batch lacked a direct numeric table-interpretation task.
correctionsMade.change: Replaced it with a two-week class table requiring calculation and comparison of increases; answer position 1 remains keyed.

correctionsMade.issue: A second tone item reduced coverage of the current ETS fact-versus-opinion topic.
correctionsMade.change: Replaced it with an astronomy-announcement fact-versus-opinion item and rewrote all four choice rationales.

correctionsMade.issue: The cited K–3 foundational-skills guide was not the closest support for a main-idea and gist scaffold.
correctionsMade.change: Changed the instructional reference to the IES grades 4–9 reading-interventions guide, which expressly covers gist and comprehension-building routines.

correctionsMade.issue: The cited K–3 foundational-skills guide was not the closest support for evidence-based comprehension questioning.
correctionsMade.change: Changed the instructional reference to the IES grades 4–9 reading-interventions guide.

correctionsMade.issue: The prompt asked for the meaning of resilient, but resilient was absent from the quoted sentence.
correctionsMade.change: Inserted resilient into the sentence so the contextual contrast directly supports the keyed meaning.

correctionsMade.issue: The cited foundational-skills guide was not the closest support for identifying gist and omitting examples in summaries.
correctionsMade.change: Changed the instructional reference to the IES grades 4–9 reading-interventions guide.

correctionsMade.issue: The cited foundational-skills guide was not the closest support for teaching problem–solution text structure.
correctionsMade.change: Changed the instructional reference to the IES K–3 reading-comprehension guide, which expressly recommends teaching organizational structure.

correctionsMade.issue: The keyed scaffold named word chunks without identifying their morphological roles and used imprecise blending language.
correctionsMade.change: Labeled trans- as a prefix, port as a root, and -ation as a suffix; clarified whole-word blending and contextual verification.

correctionsMade.issue: The cited foundational-skills guide was not the closest support for comprehension across prose, captions, and a diagram.
correctionsMade.change: Changed the supplemental instructional reference to the IES grades 4–9 reading-interventions guide; retained the ETS blueprint citation for diagrams.

correctionsMade.issue: The source-credibility research task fit writing/research more closely than the current ETS classroom-reading topics.
correctionsMade.change: Replaced it with a dictionary, syntax, and context task requiring selection and verification of the correct sense of channel.

correctionsMade.issue: The sarcasm-and-tone task was outside the explicit current ETS classroom-reading topic list.
correctionsMade.change: Replaced it with an antonym task using scarce, abundant, and the contrast signal but, while retaining a multilingual-learner context and answer position 0.

correctionsMade.issue: The cited foundational-skills guide was not the closest support for prereading prediction and gradual release.
correctionsMade.change: Changed the instructional reference to the IES K–3 reading-comprehension guide; retained the ETS citation for prereading strategies.

readingSkillsAndKnowledgeTopicsRepresent: word and phrase meaning in context

verdict: pass-with-corrections: all 34 items are approved for integration as assistant-authored independent ParaPro practice drafts; this is not official ETS content or psychometric validation.
